column2: SAP release marker lookup table
Maps (software component, release range) -> a canonical marker string used
by sap_rfc_system_info.ps1 and sap_select_vbs_variant.ps1.
First-match-wins. Rows are evaluated in file order. The marker becomes
the server_release_marker stored on each connection profile in
{work_dir}\runtime\connections.json (Phase 4.2) and serves as the
filename tag for version-specific VBS variants (e.g.,
sap_se38_update.S4HANA_2022.vbs).
Columns:
component  - SAP software component name from table CVERS
release_lo - minimum release (inclusive). Numeric string compare.
release_hi - maximum release (inclusive). Empty = open-ended.
family     - high-level family: S4HANA, ECC, SAP_BASIS, NW (for fallback)
marker     - canonical tag emitted for this match
notes      - free text
release_lo

column2: Kernel-only fallback. Used by sap_rfc_system_info.ps1 ONLY when every
CVERS lookup returned empty (e.g. user lacks S_TABU_DIS for table-class SS,
or S_RFC for RFC_READ_TABLE on function group SDTX). Kernel release
tracks SAP_BASIS one-for-one on on-premise releases, so kernel 754
strongly implies SAP_BASIS 754 -> S/4HANA 1909 OR pure NetWeaver 7.54.
The S/4 vs. NW ambiguity cannot be resolved without CVERS, so the marker
preserves both. Variant selectors that want S/4HANA-only behaviour should
glob-match the canonical prefix (S4HANA_1909_*) and accept this fallback.
